query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it empirically and theoretically investigates the impact of a specific data imputation method (conditional mean imputation) on statistical estimation properties (minimax rates, pivotality) in high-dimensional regression, which is a core component of the causal inference pipeline. Although it focuses on regression rather than explicitly labeled "causal inference," it establishes the necessary baseline for how imputation affects statistical validity, a
